escalatedRisks.risk: 🆕 WF6 H2 2026 supply gap quantified at 2,000 tons — material crisis escalation
escalatedRisks.status: CRITICAL NEW - June 13, 2026. Markets.com quantifies WF6 supply gap at 2,000 tons for H2 2026 after Kanto Denka + Central Glass permanent shutdown July 1 (~25% global capacity gone). 12-18 month qualification cycle for new suppliers means no relief until H1 2028 at earliest.
escalatedRisks.detail: This quantification transforms the WF6 crisis from 'probably severe' to 'measured and severe.' 2,000 tons represents ~8-12% of remaining global WF6 production capacity — demand will immediately exceed supply by that margin on July 1. Every semiconductor manufacturing process using CVD tungsten deposition (DRAM bit lines, NAND word lines, logic interconnects) is affected. Combined with MLC NAND exit (final 17 days), this is an unprecedented dual supply contraction for NAND manufacturing.
